basicDetails.schemeName: Ayush Oushadhi Gunvatta evam Uttpadan Samvardhan Yojana (AOGUSY): Strengthening of Central and State Regulatory Frameworks Including Technical Human Resource and Capacity Building Programs for Ayush Drugs

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme-component "Strengthening of Central and State Regulatory Frameworks Including Technical Human Resource and Capacity Building Programs for Ayush Drugs" under the "Ayush Oushadhi Gunvatta evam Uttpadan Samvardhan Yojana" implemented by the Ministry of Ayush, Government of India, focuses on improving regulatory oversight of Ayurveda, Siddha, Unani and Homoeopathy (ASU&H) drugs. At the Central level, the scheme supports the Ayush vertical under the Central Drugs Standard Control Organization (CDSCO) for professional services, training programs, documentation, dissemination of quality control materials, and upgradation of drug regulatory portals and websites. At the State/UT level, the scheme strengthens Ayush Drug Control systems by supporting regulatory and quality control activities, training of technical staff, testing of statutory and survey samples, portal maintenance, and awareness programs.
schemeContent.benefits_md: > **For Ayush vertical in CDSCO:** - **Training Support:** Training programs for capacity building are supported with a financial cap of ₹5,00,000/- per program.- **Training Support Limit:** A maximum of three training programs can be supported under this component. > **For State Ayush Directorate / State Drug Controller of Ayush drugs:** - **Support for Regulatory and Quality Control Activities:** Financial assistance up to ₹15,00,000/- per year per State/UT for regulatory and quality control activities.- **Reimbursable Expenditure Includes:** 1. Maintenance and development of online regulatory portals of Ayush Drug Controller/Licensing Authority, 1. Collection and testing of statutory and survey drug samples, 1. Training of technical staff at PCIM&H / NABL as per approved norms, 1. Awareness and IEC activities related to Ayush drug regulation.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme component aims to strengthen the regulatory framework for Ayush drugs at the Central and State/UT levels by enhancing technical manpower, capacity building, quality control systems, and regulatory infrastructure.

children.text: The scheme-component "Strengthening of Central and State Regulatory Frameworks Including Technical Human Resource and Capacity Building Programs for Ayush Drugs" under the "Ayush Oushadhi Gunvatta evam Uttpadan Samvardhan Yojana" implemented by the Ministry of Ayush, Government of India, focuses on improving regulatory oversight of Ayurveda, Siddha, Unani and Homoeopathy (ASU&H) drugs.

At the Central level, the scheme supports the Ayush vertical under the Central Drugs Standard Control Organization (CDSCO) for professional services, training programs, documentation, dissemination of quality control materials, and upgradation of drug regulatory portals and websites.

At the State/UT level, the scheme strengthens Ayush Drug Control systems by supporting regulatory and quality control activities, training of technical staff, testing of statutory and survey samples, portal maintenance, and awareness programs.
Training programs for capacity building are supported with a financial cap of ₹5,00,000/- per program.
A maximum of three training programs can be supported under this component.
For State Ayush Directorate / State Drug Controller of Ayush drugs:
Support for Regulatory and Quality Control Activities:
Financial assistance up to ₹15,00,000/- per year per State/UT for regulatory and quality control activities.
Maintenance and development of online regulatory portals of Ayush Drug Controller/Licensing Authority,
Collection and testing of statutory and survey drug samples,
Training of technical staff at PCIM&H / NABL as per approved norms,
Awareness and IEC activities related to Ayush drug regulation.

for seeking a grant-in-aid under the component.

applicationProcess.process_md: - The grantee institute/Central/State Govt. may apply in the [FORMAT –C1](https://ayush.gov.in/resources/pdf/schemes/aoushdhi.pdf) for seeking a grant-in-aid under the component.

children.text: Fill in all the mandatory details and upload the relevant documents.
Verify the captcha and click on "Register". If required, click "Cancel" to discard changes.
Enter the registered email id, password.
Type the captcha and click on "Login".

applicationProcess.process_md: > **Registration Process:** **Step 1:** Visit the [Ayush NGO Portal](https://ngo.ayush.gov.in/register). **Step 2:** Fill in all the mandatory details and upload the relevant documents. **Step 3:** Verify the captcha and click on "Register". If required, click "Cancel" to discard changes. > **Login Process:** **Step 1:** Visit the [Ayush NGO Portal](https://ngo.ayush.gov.in/login). **Step 2:** Enter the registered email id, password. **Step 3:** Type the captcha and click on "Login". - User Manual: [Click here](https://ngo.ayush.gov.in/uploads/assets/Frontend_User_Manual_v-1.2.pdf).- Contact Us: [Click here](https://ngo.ayush.gov.in/contact-us).

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: > **For Ayush vertical in CDSCO:** - The Ayush vertical under the Central Drugs Standard Control Organization (CDSCO) is eligible for financial assistance under this component. > **For State Ayush Directorate / State Drug Controller of Ayush drugs:** - State Ayush Directorate / State Drug Controller of Ayush drugs are eligible. - The state should have a functional Drug Testing Laboratory and a separate functional Enforcement Mechanism for ASU&H drugs. > **Note:** Preference is given to States/UTs maintaining a database of manufacturing units and products, implementing dossier-based licensing, and conducting regular inspections as per the [Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940](https://www.indiacode.nic.in/bitstream/123456789/15278/1/drug_cosmeticsa1940-23.pdf) and Rules, 1945.

children.text: The Ayush vertical under the Central Drugs Standard Control Organization (CDSCO) is eligible for financial assistance under this component.
For State Ayush Directorate / State Drug Controller of Ayush drugs:
State Ayush Directorate / State Drug Controller of Ayush drugs are eligible.
The state should have a functional Drug Testing Laboratory and a separate functional Enforcement Mechanism for ASU&H drugs.
